raw_description: Worker fell from scaffolding on level 8 approximately 12 feet. Worker is conscious but complaining of back pain. Called ambulance.

analysis.severity_reason: Fall from height >6 feet with potential spinal injury.
analysis.immediate_cause: Scaffolding plank gave way under worker weight.
analysis.osha_reason: Fall from height resulting in likely hospitalization.
immediate_actions_required: 911 called — ambulance en route
Secure scaffolding level 8 — no entry
Notify project superintendent
Preserve scaffolding plank for investigation
analysis.summary: Worker fell approximately 12 feet from Level 8 scaffolding. Worker remained conscious but reported back pain. Emergency services contacted immediately. Scaffolding area secured pending investigation.

raw_description: Near miss — crane swung load over workers who were not in designated safe zone. No injuries. Workers did not receive proper communication of lift schedule.

analysis.severity_reason: High potential for struck-by fatality if swing had contacted workers.
analysis.immediate_cause: Failure to communicate lift schedule to ground crew.
analysis.osha_reason: No injury occurred — near miss only.
immediate_actions_required: Review lift communication protocol
Mandatory toolbox talk with crane operator and ground crew
Re-mark exclusion zones with high-vis barriers
analysis.summary: Tower crane load swung over workers not cleared from the lift zone. No injuries sustained. Root cause identified as breakdown in pre-lift communication protocol. Corrective action initiated immediately.

raw_description: Minor cut on right hand from rebar. Treated on site with first aid kit. No stitches needed. Worker returned to work.

analysis.severity_reason: Minor laceration treated with first aid only.
analysis.immediate_cause: Unprotected rebar ends — no caps installed.
analysis.osha_reason: First aid only, worker returned to full duty.
immediate_actions_required: Install rebar end caps on all exposed rebar
Check PPE (gloves) compliance in rebar zone
analysis.summary: Worker sustained minor cut to right hand from exposed rebar end. Treated on-site with first aid. Worker returned to full duty. Rebar end caps to be installed to prevent recurrence.

raw_description: Electrical panel sparked when subcontractor attempted connection without lockout/tagout. Worker got minor shock, hand is numb. Sent to urgent care.

analysis.severity_reason: Electrical shock with numbness indicates nerve involvement — hospitalization possible.
analysis.immediate_cause: Lockout/tagout procedure not followed before energized work.
analysis.osha_reason: Medical treatment beyond first aid — sent to urgent care.
immediate_actions_required: Confirm worker received medical evaluation
Lock out electrical panel — do not energize
Notify electrical subcontractor supervisor
Conduct LOTO re-training for all electrical subs
analysis.summary: Subcontractor received electrical shock from panel while performing connection without lockout/tagout. Worker experienced hand numbness and was transported to urgent care. LOTO violation to be investigated and corrective training scheduled.

raw_description: Chemical spill — concrete form release agent spilled on worker's forearm. Skin is red and irritated. Washed with water for 15 min. No blistering.

analysis.severity_reason: Chemical burn with skin irritation requires medical evaluation.
analysis.immediate_cause: No PPE (chemical-resistant gloves) worn during product application.
analysis.osha_reason: Chemical burn requiring medical evaluation beyond first aid.
immediate_actions_required: Continue flushing with water — 20 minutes minimum
Consult SDS for release agent — check medical section
Send worker to occupational health clinic
Enforce chemical PPE compliance in formwork area
analysis.summary: Worker sustained chemical burn to forearm from form release agent spill. Immediate decontamination performed on site. Medical evaluation recommended due to persistent skin irritation. PPE compliance failure identified as root cause.

raw_description: Sub-contractor got shocked while connecting to the panel on floor 3. Didn't follow lockout tagout procedure. His hand went numb. We sent him to urgent care right away.

analysis.severity_reason: Classified as CRITICAL based on keywords detected in report.
analysis.immediate_cause: Under investigation — see full incident description.
analysis.osha_reason: Likely requires medical treatment beyond first aid.
analysis.risk_score_reason: Risk scored mainly from CRITICAL severity plus incident category (Electrical) and OSHA recordable.
evidence_snippets: Sub-contractor got shocked while connecting to the panel on floor 3
analysis.environmental_hazard_reason: Environmental hazard assessed as low based on incident category and environmental-release indicators.
sustainability_concerns: Incorrect energizing/repair triggers avoidable component replacement (e-waste + materials waste)
sustainability_actions_required: Strengthen LOTO compliance (pre-task checklist + sign-off + verification point at the panel/work location)
Reduce reactive repairs by adding failure-pattern checks to preventive maintenance schedules
Stop unnecessary replacements: repair/replace decisions require documented fault verification
Ensure any removed components are handled as e-waste/material waste per approved vendor stream
analysis.efficiency_score_reason: Efficiency opportunity driven by high-impact incident impact (Electrical) and process-control gaps detected in the report.
efficiency_actions_required: Use a LOTO verification checklist at the point of work (checkboxes + supervisor sign-off before energizing)
Add photo evidence/verification steps for completed lockout/tagout where feasible
Track repeat issues and trigger targeted re-training within 7 days of the incident
Ensure spare parts are kitted to reduce downtime from follow-on repairs
immediate_actions_required: Secure the area and prevent further hazards
Provide first aid or call 911 if needed
Notify safety manager immediately
Preserve evidence for investigation
analysis.summary: Incident report submitted. AI analysis completed using rule-based fallback (no API key configured). Incident classified as Electrical with CRITICAL severity. Please review and verify all fields.
